title: Synchronize every clock-domain crossing
description: Treat any signal crossing clock domains as a metastability hazard needing a synchronizer or FIFO plus timing constraints, never a bare wire.
body: Use when a signal generated in one clock crosses into another. Do not connect cross-domain signals directly, and do not synchronize a multi-bit bus with parallel 2-flop synchronizers (bits resolve at different times). Actions: use a 2+ flop synchronizer for single-bit control, a gray-coded pointer or async FIFO for buses, and add false-path/max-delay constraints. Validation signals include a CDC report with every crossing covered and MTBF analysis. Risk boundary: metastability failures are probabilistic and field-only; a removed CDC warning is not a fix unless a real synchronizer was added.
